words.definitionEn: a person that you are doing an activity with, such as dancing or playing a game
words.exampleEn: a dance/tennis partner

words.definitionEn: a social occasion, often in a person’s home, at which people eat, drink, talk, dance and enjoy themselves
words.exampleEn: a dinner/tea/cocktail party

words.definitionEn: an official document that identifies you as a citizen of a particular country, and that you may have to show when you enter or leave a country
words.exampleEn: a valid passport

words.definitionEn: gone by in time
words.exampleEn: in past years/centuries

words.definitionEn: the time that has gone by; things that happened in an earlier time
words.exampleEn: in the past, I used to go there often in the past.

words.definitionEn: later than something
words.exampleEn: half past two

words.definitionEn: to give somebody money for work, goods, services, etc.
words.exampleEn: Who's paying?

words.definitionEn: an instrument made of plastic or metal used for writing with ink (= coloured liquid for writing, etc.)
words.exampleEn: an ink pen

words.definitionEn: a narrow piece of wood, or a metal or plastic case, containing a black or coloured substance, used for drawing or writing
words.exampleEn: I'll get a pencil and paper.

words.definitionEn: human beings; men, women and children
words.exampleEn: At least ten people were killed in the crash.

words.definitionEn: a powder made from dried berries (called peppercorns), used to give a hot, spicy taste to food
words.exampleEn: Season with salt and pepper

words.definitionEn: having everything that is necessary; complete and without faults or weaknesses
words.exampleEn: in perfect condition

words.definitionEn: a particular length of time
words.exampleEn: a long/an extended period

words.definitionEn: a human as an individual
words.exampleEn: What sort of person would do a thing like that?

words.definitionEn: your own; not belonging to or connected with anyone else
words.exampleEn: The novel is written from personal experience.

words.definitionEn: a piece of equipment for talking to people who are not in the same place as you
words.exampleEn: I have to make a phone call.

words.definitionEn: to make a phone call to somebody
words.exampleEn: I was just phoning up for a chat.

words.definitionEn: a picture that is made by using a camera that stores images in digital form or that has a film sensitive to light inside it
words.exampleEn: a colour/black-and-white photo

words.definitionEn: a picture that is made by using a camera that stores images in digital form or that has a film sensitive to light inside it
words.exampleEn: aerial photographs of the crash site

words.definitionEn: a group of words that have a particular meaning when used together
words.exampleEn: a memorable/catchy phrase

words.definitionEn: a large musical instrument played by pressing the black and white keys on the keyboard. The sound is produced by small hammers hitting the metal strings inside the piano.
words.exampleEn: to play the piano

words.definitionEn: a painting or drawing, etc. that shows a scene, a person or thing
words.exampleEn: He likes books with lots of pictures in them.

words.definitionEn: an amount of something that has been cut or separated from the rest of it; a standard amount of something
words.exampleEn: piece of something, She wrote something on a small piece of paper.

words.definitionEn: an animal with pink, black or brown skin, short legs, a broad nose and a short curly tail. Pigs are kept on farms for their meat (called pork) or live in the wild.
words.exampleEn: a pig farmer

words.definitionEn: pale red in colour
words.exampleEn: pale pink roses

words.definitionEn: the colour that is produced when you mix red and white together
words.exampleEn: She was dressed in pink.

words.definitionEn: a particular position, point or area
words.exampleEn: in a place, Keep your purse in a safe place.

words.definitionEn: something that you intend to do or achieve
words.exampleEn: plan for something, Do you have any plans for the summer?

words.definitionEn: to make detailed arrangements for something you want to do in the future
words.exampleEn: plan something, to plan a trip

words.definitionEn: a flying vehicle with wings and one or more engines
words.exampleEn: a passenger/fighter/military/private plane

words.definitionEn: the 8th month of the year, between July and September
words.exampleEn: She was born in August.

words.definitionEn: a living thing that grows in the earth and usually has a stem, leaves and roots, especially one that is smaller than a tree or bush
words.exampleEn: All plants need light and water.

words.definitionEn: a piece of writing performed by actors in a theatre or on television or radio
words.exampleEn: a stage/radio play

words.definitionEn: to do things for pleasure, as children do; to enjoy yourself, rather than work
words.exampleEn: You'll have to play inside today.

words.definitionEn: the sister of your father or mother; the wife of your uncle or aunt
words.exampleEn: Aunt Alice

words.definitionEn: a person who takes part in a game or sport
words.exampleEn: a football/tennis/chess player

words.definitionEn: used as a polite way of asking for something or telling somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: Please sit down.

words.definitionEn: a thing that somebody says or writes giving their opinion or stating a fact
words.exampleEn: She made several interesting points in the article.

words.definitionEn: an official organization whose job is to make people obey the law and to prevent and solve crime; the people who work for this organization
words.exampleEn: Get out of the house or I'll call the police.

words.definitionEn: a male police officer
words.exampleEn: a plain-clothes/uniformed policeman

words.definitionEn: an area of water that has been created for people to swim in
words.exampleEn: an indoor/outdoor pool

words.definitionEn: having very little money; not having enough money for basic needs
words.exampleEn: They were too poor to buy shoes for the kids.

words.definitionEn: liked or enjoyed by a large number of people
words.exampleEn: Andalucia is a popular tourist destination.

words.definitionEn: good or useful
words.exampleEn: The tests have so far yielded positive results.

words.definitionEn: that can be done or achieved
words.exampleEn: possible to do something, New technology has made it possible to communicate more easily.

words.definitionEn: the official system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.
words.exampleEn: by post, I'll send the original to you by post.

words.definitionEn: to send a letter, etc. to somebody by post
words.exampleEn: post something, The cost of posting a letter has gone up again.

words.definitionEn: a round white vegetable with a brown or red skin that grows underground as part of a plant also called a potato
words.exampleEn: Will you peel the potatoes for me?

words.definitionEn: the unit of money in the UK, worth 100 pence
words.exampleEn: a ten-pound note

words.definitionEn: doing an activity or training regularly so that you can improve your skill; the time you spend doing this
words.exampleEn: conversation practice

words.definitionEn: to do an activity or train regularly so that you can improve your skill
words.exampleEn: You need to practise every day.

words.definitionEn: the season of the year between summer and winter, when leaves change colour and the weather becomes colder
words.exampleEn: in the autumn of something, in the autumn of 2010

words.definitionEn: to like one thing or person better than another; to choose one thing rather than something else because you like it better
words.exampleEn: prefer something, ‘Coffee or tea?’ ‘I'd prefer tea, thanks.’

words.definitionEn: to make something or somebody ready to be used or to do something
words.exampleEn: prepare something/somebody, to prepare a report

words.definitionEn: existing or happening now
words.exampleEn: I am not satisfied with the present situation.

words.definitionEn: a thing that you give to somebody as a gift
words.exampleEn: What can I get him for a birthday present?

words.definitionEn: attractive without being very beautiful
words.exampleEn: a pretty face

words.definitionEn: to some extent; fairly
words.exampleEn: The game was pretty good.

words.definitionEn: the amount of money that you have to pay for something
words.exampleEn: Boat for sale, price £8 000

words.definitionEn: used to say that something is likely to happen or to be true
words.exampleEn: You're probably right.

words.definitionEn: a thing that is difficult to deal with or to understand
words.exampleEn: big/serious/major problems

words.definitionEn: a thing that is grown, produced or created, usually for sale
words.exampleEn: food/agricultural/software products

words.definitionEn: something that people watch on television or listen to on the radio
words.exampleEn: a news programme

words.definitionEn: a piece of work involving careful study of a subject over a period of time, done by school or college students
words.exampleEn: a history project

words.definitionEn: to or at a distance from somebody/something in space or time
words.exampleEn: The beach is a mile away.

words.definitionEn: a very young child or animal
words.exampleEn: The baby's crying!

words.definitionEn: to or into the place, condition, situation or activity where somebody/something was before
words.exampleEn: When is he coming back to work?

words.definitionEn: the part of the human body that is on the opposite side to the chest, between the neck and the tops of the legs; the part of an animal’s body that corresponds to this
words.exampleEn: on your back, He was lying on his back on the sofa.

words.definitionEn: having the colour of blue and red mixed together
words.exampleEn: a purple flower/dress

words.definitionEn: the colour of blue and red mixed together
words.exampleEn: Her hair was dyed a bright shade of purple.

words.definitionEn: to move something into a particular place or position
words.exampleEn: Put the cases down there, please.

words.definitionEn: one of four equal parts of something
words.exampleEn: quarter of something, a quarter of an hour/a century

words.definitionEn: a sentence, phrase or word that asks for information
words.exampleEn: You didn't answer my question.

words.definitionEn: done with speed; taking or lasting a short time
words.exampleEn: a quick look/check/search

words.definitionEn: fast
words.exampleEn: She walked quickly away.

words.definitionEn: making very little noise
words.exampleEn: her quiet voice

words.definitionEn: to some degree
words.exampleEn: quite good/interesting/common/difficult

words.definitionEn: the activity of broadcasting programmes for people to listen to; the programmes that are broadcast
words.exampleEn: The play was written specially for radio.

words.definitionEn: water that falls from the sky in separate drops
words.exampleEn: There will be rain in all parts tomorrow.

words.definitionEn: when it rains, water falls from the sky in drops
words.exampleEn: Is it raining?

words.definitionEn: unpleasant; full of problems
words.exampleEn: I have some bad news for you, I'm afraid.

words.definitionEn: to look at and understand the meaning of written or printed words or symbols
words.exampleEn: She's still learning to read.

words.definitionEn: a person who reads, especially one who reads a lot or in a particular way
words.exampleEn: an avid reader of science fiction

words.definitionEn: the activity of somebody who reads
words.exampleEn: My hobbies include reading and painting.

words.definitionEn: fully prepared for what you are going to do and able to start it immediately
words.exampleEn: Just a minute—I'm almost ready.

words.definitionEn: actually existing or happening and not imagined or pretended
words.exampleEn: a real danger/risk/threat/concern

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize an adjective or adverb
words.exampleEn: That's a really good idea.

words.definitionEn: a cause or an explanation for something that has happened or that somebody has done
words.exampleEn: He said no but he didn't give a reason.

words.definitionEn: a container made of cloth, leather, plastic or paper, used to carry things in, especially when shopping or travelling
words.exampleEn: He was carrying a heavy bag of groceries.

words.definitionEn: having the colour of blood or fire
words.exampleEn: bright/light/dark red lipstick

words.definitionEn: the colour of blood or fire
words.exampleEn: She often wears red.

words.definitionEn: a round object used for throwing, hitting or kicking in games and sports
words.exampleEn: a golf/tennis/bowling/soccer ball

words.definitionEn: to rest while you are doing something that you enjoy, especially after work or effort
words.exampleEn: Just relax and enjoy the movie.

words.definitionEn: to have or keep an image in your memory of an event, a person, a place, etc. from the past
words.exampleEn: remember somebody/something, This is Carla. Do you remember her?

words.definitionEn: to say or write something again or more than once
words.exampleEn: repeat something, to repeat a question/a claim/an assertion/a warning

words.definitionEn: a written or spoken account of an event, especially one that is published or broadcast
words.exampleEn: Are these news reports true?

words.definitionEn: a long curved fruit with a thick yellow skin and that is soft inside, which grows on trees in hot countries
words.exampleEn: a bunch of bananas

words.definitionEn: a small group of musicians who play popular music together, often with a singer or singers
words.exampleEn: a rock/punk/jazz band

words.definitionEn: an organization that provides various financial services, for example keeping or lending money
words.exampleEn: I don't have much money in the bank at the end of the month.

words.definitionEn: a place where you can buy and eat a meal
words.exampleEn: We're going to try this Italian restaurant that just opened.

words.definitionEn: a thing that is caused or produced because of something else
words.exampleEn: And did your intervention produce the desired result?

words.definitionEn: the action of arriving in or coming back to a place that you were in before
words.exampleEn: return of somebody to…, A week had passed since their return to Geneva.

words.definitionEn: to come or go back from one place to another
words.exampleEn: I waited a long time for him to return.

words.definitionEn: short, narrow white or brown grain grown on wet land in hot countries as food; the plant that produces this grain
words.exampleEn: a grain of rice

words.definitionEn: having a lot of money or property
words.exampleEn: She's one of the richest women in the world.

words.definitionEn: to sit on an animal, especially a horse, and control it as it moves
words.exampleEn: I learnt to ride as a child.

words.definitionEn: true or correct as a fact
words.exampleEn: Did you get the answer right?

words.definitionEn: on or to the right side
words.exampleEn: Turn right at the end of the street.

words.definitionEn: the right side or direction
words.exampleEn: on the right, Take the first street on the right.

words.definitionEn: a natural flow of water that continues in a long line across land to the sea
words.exampleEn: Eventually we came to the mouth of the River Thames.

words.definitionEn: a hard surface built for vehicles to travel on
words.exampleEn: a main/major/minor road

words.definitionEn: a part of a building that has its own walls, floor and ceiling and is usually used for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: I heard him enter the room.

words.definitionEn: the normal order and way in which you regularly do things
words.exampleEn: to settle/get/fall into a routine

words.definitionEn: a statement of what may, must or must not be done in a particular situation or when playing a game
words.exampleEn: She laid down strict rules for her tenants, including prompt payment of rent.

words.definitionEn: to move using your legs, going faster than when you walk
words.exampleEn: Can you run as fast as Mike?

words.definitionEn: unhappy or showing unhappiness
words.exampleEn: sad to do something, We are very sad to hear that you are leaving.

words.definitionEn: a mixture of raw vegetables such as lettuce, tomato and cucumber, usually served with other food as part of a meal
words.exampleEn: All main courses come with salad or vegetables.

words.definitionEn: a white substance that is added to food to make it taste better or to preserve it. Salt is obtained from mines and is also found in seawater. It is sometimes called common salt to show that it is different from other chemical salts. Its chemical name is sodium chloride.
words.exampleEn: Pass the salt, please.

words.definitionEn: exactly the one or ones referred to or mentioned; not different
words.exampleEn: We have lived in the same house for twenty years.

words.definitionEn: in the same way
words.exampleEn: We treat boys exactly the same as girls.

words.definitionEn: the same thing or things
words.exampleEn: I would do the same again.

words.definitionEn: two slices of bread, often spread with butter, with a layer of meat, cheese, etc. between them
words.exampleEn: a ham/tuna/egg sandwich

words.definitionEn: the day of the week after Friday and before Sunday
words.exampleEn: It's Saturday today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: to speak or tell somebody something, using words
words.exampleEn: + speech, ‘Hello!’ she said.

words.definitionEn: a place where children go to be educated
words.exampleEn: My brother and I went to the same school.

words.definitionEn: knowledge about the structure and behaviour of the natural and physical world, based on facts that you can prove, for example by experiments
words.exampleEn: new developments in science and technology

words.definitionEn: a person who studies one or more of the natural sciences (= for example, physics, chemistry or biology)
words.exampleEn: a research scientist

words.definitionEn: the salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface and surrounds its continents and islands
words.exampleEn: The waste was dumped in the sea.

words.definitionEn: happening or coming next after the first in a series of similar things or people; 2nd
words.exampleEn: This is the second time it's happened.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring time. There are 60 seconds in one minute.
words.exampleEn: in… seconds, She can run 100 metres in just over 11 seconds.

words.definitionEn: happening or coming next after the first in a series of similar things or people; 2nd
words.exampleEn: This is the second time it's happened.

words.definitionEn: a large, long container that you put water in and then get into to wash your whole body
words.exampleEn: I'm in the bath!

words.definitionEn: any of the parts into which something is divided
words.exampleEn: section of something, That section of the road is still closed.

words.definitionEn: a room in which there is a bath, a washbasin and often a toilet
words.exampleEn: Go and wash your hands in the bathroom.

words.definitionEn: to become aware of somebody/something by using your eyes
words.exampleEn: see somebody/something, She looked for him but couldn't see him in the crowd.
